taskDescription: Click on the key endocrine terms in the text below.
textField:
The *hypothalamus* is the master regulator of the endocrine system. It secretes *releasing* and *inhibiting* hormones that travel through the *hypothalamo-hypophyseal* *portal* system to control the *anterior* *pituitary* gland. The posterior pituitary stores and releases *oxytocin* and *ADH*, which are synthesised in the hypothalamus.

The *thyroid* gland produces *T3* and *T4* from *tyrosine* residues on *thyroglobulin*. This process requires *iodine* and the enzyme *thyroid* *peroxidase*. *TSH* from the anterior pituitary stimulates thyroid hormone synthesis, while elevated T3 and T4 exert *negative* *feedback* on TSH release.

The *adrenal* cortex has three zones: the zona *glomerulosa* produces *aldosterone*, the zona *fasciculata* produces *cortisol*, and the zona *reticularis* produces androgens. The adrenal *medulla* secretes *catecholamines* including *adrenaline* and *noradrenaline* under sympathetic stimulation.

overallFeedback.feedback: Review endocrine physiology and try again.
